title: Samantha and the Ship of Stars
author: Dady Fredy
subtitle: A Bedtime Story by Dady Fredy
dedication: For Samantha — small in size, but rare.

lines: Star after star,
sun after sun…
Love is the light
that outshines every one.

pages.title: Samantha and the Ship of Stars
pages.subtitle: A Bedtime Story by Dady Fredy
pages.dedication: For Samantha — small in size, but rare.

lines: One quiet night in Serbia's sky,
Samantha watched the stars drift by.
"Dady Fredy," she softly said,
"Where do the farthest star paths lead?"
He smiled and whispered, "Close your eyes—
Tonight we sail beyond the skies."
A silver ship called Svetlost came,
Which means 'the Light' — a Serbian name.
She hummed a sound like velvet wings,
No roar of engines, just soft rings.
Her dashboard glowed with gentle gold:
"Climb aboard — the stars are cold."
Samantha first, then Dady too —
The door slid shut and off they flew.
Past Earth so blue, past silver Moon,
Past Mars who whistled a rusty tune.
They waved at Saturn's icy rings,
Where tiny moons rode diamond swings.
Then Jupiter — so wide and grand —
Blew storms like music from a band.
The planets faded, one by one,
The Milky Way began to run
Like spilled srebrna — Serbian silver —
A stream of light that made them shiver.
Then out beyond — a shining sea!
More galaxies than eyes could see.
Some spun like pinwheels, arms spread wide,
Some glowed like lanterns, deep inside.
They drifted through a cloud of dust
Where baby stars were born — a gust
Of pink and gold lit up the dark,
Each newborn sun a tiny spark.
"Each galaxy," Dady said low,
"Is a city where billions of stars grow.
Perhaps somewhere a girl like you
Is watching from a window, too."
"Dady Fredy," she whispered tight,
"Are we small in all this light?"
He held her close and said with care,
"Small in size — but, anak, rare.
The universe is wild and wide,
But love? Love travels right inside.
No distance, darkness, time, or space
Can keep it from its resting place."
Then Svetlost softly turned for home,
Back through the quiet stars they roamed.
The Milky Way returned in view,
Their little solar system, too.
Past Jupiter, past Saturn's rings,
Past Mars still whistling rusty things.
Past Moon, through clouds, through Serbia's air,
And down to bed — so calm, so fair.
And Dady Fredy tucked her tight,
And whispered the words he says each night:
"Wherever you wander, anak, however far—
My love will find you past every star."
So close your eyes now, my Samantha, my dear,
Svetlost is resting — the stars are all here.
The galaxies hum and the universe bends—
And Dady Fredy's love never, ever ends.
Goodnight, Samantha.
Goodnight, anak.
